titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in 792
GA 792
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: υμεις at end of 8.14 moved to v. 15 by David Parker.
Block marker 8.13 between 5.30 and 5.32 replaced with 5.31 and note added by David Parker.
υμιν deleted at 18.39 by David Parker.
ουν added at 18.26 by David Parker.
Correction added at 14.4 by David Parker.
χειμαρου changed to χειμαρουν by David Parker.
ει moved from 18.5 to 18.8 by Rachel Kevern.
Word break corrected at 8.39 by Rachel Kevern.
Word break corrected at 8.21 by Rachel Kevern.
Word break corrected at 8.13-1 and 8.53 by Rachel Kevern.
ης corrected to η ο and ο οχλοις changed to οοχλοις at 9.16 by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 9.4 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
3-5 line gap changed to 305 char gap at 1.27 by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 13.24 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 20.17 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 19.38 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 15.4 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 8.6 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 14.13 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
θυσ(ης) changed to θυσ(ω) at 13.37 by Rachel Kevern.
εις σελθειν changed to εισελθεν at 13.27 and word break changed at 14.23 by Rachel Kevern.
Extra initial letter ε added to ενεβριμησατο plus editorial note at 11.33 added by Rachel Kevern.
μετ αυτα changed to μετα υτα at 13.7 by Rachel Kevern.
Word break changed at 11.28 by David Parker.
Word break in 13.25 corrected by David Parker.
υ in αυτους at 7.45 dotted by David Parker.
Word break in 9.38 corrected by David Parker.
Adjusted to XML specifications 1.4 by Hugh Houghton.
Converted from plain text by Catherine Smith using Python conversion scripts 1.0.

body.note: Status:G-A Number: 04_307920Name of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: 87vBase text used: TRns Transcribed by: E. PanouTranscription begun: 1 Feb 2012Transcription finished: 8 Feb 2012 Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate): Lectionary annotations - No Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes Diples (> or >> in the margin next to certain text, indicating that text is a quotation from the Old Testament) - No Are there any special symbols in the margin next to any of the following verses: 5.4 No 7.53 - 8.11 No Other remarks: The quality of images makes the reading of words or letters (of the verso folios usually) extremely difficult. Heavily abbreviated. Numerous spelling mistakes, although the same word is sometimes mispelled and other times it is not, even in the same line. The scriber replaces almost everywhere the word αυτω with αυτoν. He often omits words from a verse when the meaning of the phrase does not change or merges two verses for the same reason. He sometimes repeats the last letter of a line in the beginning of the following line and rarely the same word is found in the end of a line and in the beginning of the following verse. Towards the end of the text (last ten folios) the spelling mistakes are multiplied and when a word has two similar consonants such as in the word θαλαssα he removes one of the two ss, maybe in an effort to save space. FINAL plain text transcription date: 24.04.2103

ab.note: The manuscript replaces the normal content of this verse with wording from 8.13-14.
Here we have an ordinary initial letter and an enlarged addition.
